title: Bottlenecks
description: A factory bottleneck is the stage or resource that caps overall you-agent-factory throughput even when other stages have spare capacity.
openingSummary: A factory bottleneck is the limiting stage or resource that caps overall you-agent-factory throughput even when other stages still have spare capacity.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: A factory bottleneck is the stage or resource that limits overall you-agent-factory throughput even when other stages have spare capacity.

whyItMatters.title: Why It Matters
whyItMatters.body: Finding the bottleneck focuses capacity and configuration changes on the stage that actually caps the factory, instead of adding agents where spare capacity already exists.

simpleExample.title: Simple Example
simpleExample.body: Many workers sit idle while a saturated task queue holds ready work. The queue is the bottleneck: end-to-end throughput cannot rise until that waiting backlog drains faster.

whereItAppears.title: Where It Appears
whereItAppears.body: Factory bottlenecks show up in queues, worker or workstation capacity, harness and tool latency, shared resources, and token or context pressure during long-running runs.

commonConfusions.title: Common Confusions
commonConfusions.body: A factory bottleneck is not a hardware-only roofline essay and not an SPC control chart. It names the limiting stage that caps factory throughput.
